course_description: This course will build on the foundations of the comprehensive examination of the healthy mouth and introduce the student to the practice of minimal intervention dentistry. The course will use simulated learning environments including pre-clinical laboratories to provide students with the opportunity to attain competency in the clinical skills required to provide preventive dental care and simple dental restorations. Students will also develop their clinical skills in a clinical environment and will begin seeing patients in the second semester. The focus is the dental management of healthy adults.

course_aims: This course aims to develop a substantial and in-depth appreciation of the connection and application of science to clinical dental practice, and to develop the procedural skills required to begin clinical practice of evidence-based minimal intervention dentistry.

learning_outcomes.description: Apply knowledge of oral diseases to explain clinical observations and to solve clinical problems.
Demonstrate competence in the procedural skills required for the practice of minimal intervention dentistry.
Demonstrate an applied knowledge of dental material properties and their application to direct restorative techniques.
Appropriately select and use instrumentation and dental materials for the practice of minimal intervention dentistry.
Apply the principles of clinical communication, behaviour management and oral health education to the clinical setting.
Demonstrate professional conduct, including while working with other members of the dental team.

assessment_details.title: Assessment Item 1 – RiPPLE Quiz
assessment_details.due_date: 12/08/2024 This will be conducted during your clinical coaching session.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This 40-minute quiz consists of multiple-choice questions. Topics may include: Oral diseases Dental material properties and applications Restorative techniques Knowledge of laboratory skills and procedures Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Students will complete this task via the RiPPLE active learning tool.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Late submission
You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .

assessment_details.title: Assessment Item 2 – Restorative Practical
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This is a series of three (3) pre-clinical assessments across a range of weeks within the semester. In each pre-clinical assessment, you will be given an allocated time frame within your pre-clinic time to complete a specified number of of cavity preparations of a certain class (I-V) on the teeth identified to you. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessment_details.title: Assessment Item 3 – Case Portfolio
assessment_details.task_description: In this task, you are required to construct a preclinical case portfolio of 1,400-1,600 words based on one of the Virtual Patients (VP) introduced throughout the course and related to a dental discipline of your choice. This assessment will be submitted via a link on your course Blackboard site, via Turnitin. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .

assessment_details.title: Assessment Item 4 – Viva Voce
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This 15-minute viva voce exam will be assessed by two examiners and students will be expected to answer questions, draw and explain diagrams / models, perform short practical tasks and demonstrate concepts by role-playing. learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: Clinical coaching Clinical coaching sessions are run twice weekly to complement your online SDL modules on UQ Extend. Lessons include: Glass Ionomer Cements Bonding to enamel and dentine Composite resin Amalgam, liners and bases Fundamentals of placement Principles of cavity design Principles of caries removal Vital Pulp Therapy: Pulp capping Pulpal and periapical diagnosis Applied neurophysiology Armamentarium for LA Local anaesthetic selection & techniques Complications of local anaesthesia Radiographic anatomy Extraoral radiography techniques Intraoral radiography techniques Intraoral radiographic interpretation Medical alerts Care planning for medically complex patients I Care planning for medically complex patients II Care planning for paediatric & geriatric patients Case scenarios & viva voce preparation Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: Pre-clinic There are three (3) pre-clinic sessions each week running for three (3) hours each where you will hone you practical skills in cavity preparations and restorative dentistry. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: Peer clinic There is one (1) peer clinic session per week with peer-to-peer activities preparing you for the patient clinic. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
